session.user:
Search the codebase for any existing `@json_output` decorator pattern or `emit_json` function. Also check if PR #9013 has been merged by looking at the git log or checking if the `erk.cli.json_output` module exists anywhere. Search broadly: 1. `grep -r "json_output" src/` 2. `grep -r "emit_json" src/` 3. Check if there's a `json_output` module anywhere under src/ 4. Check the content of references to json_output in `src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py` and `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/impl_init.py`
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
session.assistant: I'll help you search for the `@json_output` decorator pattern and related functionality. Let me start by checking the documentation index and then systematically search the codebase.

**[activation-scripts.md](activation-scripts.md)** — working with worktree environment setup, understanding .erk/activate.sh scripts, configuring post-create commands 7→- **[agent-friendly-cli.md](agent-friendly-cli.md)** — adding --json flag to a CLI command, creating or modifying MCP tools, implementing structured JSON output for CLI commands, working on agent-friendly CLI patterns, implementing erk schema command 8→- **[ambiguity-resolution.md](ambiguity-resolution.md)** — implementing CLI commands that accept identifiers with multiple possible semantics 12→- **[checkout-helpers.md](checkout-helpers.md)** — writing checkout commands, creating worktrees in checkout commands, implementing branch checkout logic 13→- **[checkout-teleport-split.md](checkout-teleport-split.md)** — working with erk pr checkout or erk pr teleport commands, understanding the difference between checkout and teleport, modifying cmux-open-pr command 14→- **[checkout-three-path-logic.md](checkout-three-path-logic.md)** — modifying erk br co, working with --for-plan flag, changing slot allocation behavior 15→- **[ci-aware-commands.md](ci-aware-commands.md)** — implementing commands that behave differently in CI, checking if code is running in GitHub Actions, skipping user-interactive steps in automated environments 16→- **[cli-options-validation.md](cli-options-validation.md)** — adding new CLI options or flags, implementing option validation logic, encountering unvalidated user input 17→- **[click-framework-conventions.md](click-framework-conventions.md)** — adding Click options to erk CLI commands, understanding default=None convention in Click options, distinguishing 'not provided' from 'empty string' in CLI arguments 18→- **[click-patterns.md](click-patterns.md)** — implementing CLI options with complex behavior, creating flags that optionally accept values, designing CLI flags 1→--- 2→title: "Agent-Friendly CLI Design Principles" 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding --json flag to a CLI command" 5→ - "creating or modifying MCP tools" 6→ - "implementing structured JSON output for CLI commands" 7→ - "working on agent-friendly CLI patterns" 8→ - "implementing erk schema command" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "adding --json flag to a command without using @json_output decorator" 11→ warning: "Use the shared @json_output decorator from src/erk/cli/json_output.py. Do not manually implement JSON serialization in individual commands." 12→ - action: "returning non-zero exit code when --json flag is active" 13→ warning: "JSON mode must always exit 0. Errors are communicated via {success: false, error_type, message} in stdout JSON, not via exit codes." 14→ - action: "naming a JSON output flag --json-output instead of --json" 15→ warning: "Standardized flag name is --json (not --json-output). See agent-friendly-cli.md for the naming decision." 16→ - action: "emitting human-readable text to stdout when --json is active" 17→ warning: "When --json is active, stdout must contain only the JSON result object. Human output goes to stderr via user_output()." 18→ - action: "creating an MCP tool that parses human-readable CLI output" 19→ warning: "MCP tools must call CLI commands with --json flag and parse structured JSON. Never parse human-readable text output." 20→--- 21→ 22→# Agent-Friendly CLI Design Principles 23→ 24→Principles and patterns for making erk's CLI agent-friendly — enabling MCP tools, Claude Code skills, and other AI agents to invoke erk commands and parse structured responses. 25→ 26→Derived from analysis of: 27→ 28→- [Justin Poehnelt's "Rewrite Your CLI for AI Agents"](https://justin.poehnelt.com/posts/rewrite-your-cli-for-ai-agents/) 29→- The [Google Workspace CLI (gws)](https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli) reference implementation 30→- Erk's existing CLI patterns and MCP server
... [318 lines omitted] ...
44→- Error → `{"success": false, "error_type": "machine_readable", "message": "Human-readable"}` on stdout, exit 0 57→- When active + error: catches `UserFacingCliError`, emits structured JSON error, exits 0 64→### 2. Structured JSON Errors 66→All JSON-mode errors follow the exec script contract: 71→ "error_type": "auth_required", 78→- `error_type` is machine-readable, snake_case (e.g., `auth_required`, `invalid_argument`, `not_found`) 81→- `UserFacingCliError` gains an `error_type` field (default `None` for backwards compat) 200→### Error Response 205→ "error_type": "snake_case_category", 206→ "message": "Human-readable error description" 224→3. Errors are communicated in the JSON body, not via exit codes 225→4. `error_type` is machine-readable, suitable for switch/match statements 248→def exit_with_error(error_type: str, message: str) -> NoReturn: 249→ error_json = json.dumps( 250→ {"success": False, "error_type": error_type, "message": message}, 253→ click.echo(error_json) 257→This is the exact error contract we're extending to top-level commands. 298→| `src/erk/cli/ensure.py` | **Modify**: Add `error_type` to `UserFacingCliError` | 302→| `src/erk/cli/script_output.py` | Existing JSON error pattern to align with | 313→- **Structured JSON errors** with `error_type`, `message`, `reason` fields, plus actionable hints (e.g., "enable API at this URL") 326→- `src/error.rs` - Structured JSON error handling

1→--- 2→title: CLI Output Styling Guide 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "styling CLI output" 7→ - "using colors in CLI" 8→ - "formatting terminal output" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "using click.confirm() after user_output()" 11→ warning: "Use ctx.console.confirm() for testability, or user_confirm() if no context available. Direct click.confirm() after user_output() causes buffering hangs because stderr isn't flushed." 12→ - action: "displaying user-provided text in Rich CLI tables" 13→ warning: "Use `escape_markup(value)` for user data. Brackets like `[text]` are interpreted as Rich style tags and will disappear." 14→ - action: "writing multi-line error messages in Ensure method calls" 15→ warning: "Use implicit string concatenation with \n at end of first string. Line 1 is the primary error, line 2+ is remediation context. Do NOT use \n\n (double newline) — Ensure handles spacing." 16→ - action: "using Python format strings with :N width specifiers for CLI output containing emoji" 17→ warning: "Use Rich tables instead — emoji have variable terminal widths (typically 2 cells) which break fixed-width alignment. See the Rich Tables for Variable-Width Characters section below." 18→--- 19→ 20→# CLI Output Styling Guide 21→ 22→This guide defines the standard color scheme, emoji conventions, and output abstraction patterns for erk CLI commands. 23→ 24→## Color Conventions 25→ 26→Use consistent colors and styling for CLI output via `click.style()`: 27→ 28→| Element | Color | Bold | Example | 29→| ------------------------ | ---------------- | ---- | --------------------------------------------------- | 30→| Branch names | `yellow` | No | `click.style(branch, fg="yellow")` |
... [934 lines omitted] ...
39→| Error states | `red` | No | `click.style("Error", fg="red")` | 114→- The command is long/complex and manual copying would be error-prone 137→- Unsupported terminals silently ignore the sequence (no errors) 151→- `❌` - Errors/failures 378→| Error messages | `user_output()` | User info, goes to stderr | 392→user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Branch not found") 409→user_output("Warning: This operation is destructive!") 422→user_output("Warning: This operation is destructive!") 434→user_output("Warning: This operation is destructive!") 444→- `src/erk/cli/commands/stack/consolidate_cmd.py` - Uses user_output() for error messages 446→## Error Message Guidelines 448→Use the `Ensure` class (from `erk.cli.ensure`) for all CLI validation errors. This provides consistent error styling and messaging. 450→### Error Message Format 452→All error messages should follow these principles: 465→**DO NOT** include "Error: " prefix - the `Ensure` class adds it automatically in red. 467→### Multi-line Error Messages 469→For errors that need both a primary message and remediation context, use implicit string concatenation with `
`: 481→- Line 1: Primary error description (concise, specific) 485→**DO NOT** use `

` (double newline) — the Ensure class already handles spacing in its error output formatting. 487→This pattern was established in the admin.py migration (PR #6860) and applies to all Ensure method calls where the error benefits from remediation context. 493→| `"Configuration file not found at ~/.erk/config.yml - Run 'erk init' to create it"` | `"Error: Config missing"` | 494→| `"Worktree already exists at path {path} - Use --force to overwrite or choose a different name"` | `"Error: Path exists"` | 496→| `"No child branches found - Already at the top of the stack"` | `"Validation failed"` | 500→| Situation | Error Message Template | 508→### UserFacingCliError for Mid-Function Errors 510→For errors that occur mid-function where `Ensure` precondition checks don't fit, use `UserFacingCliError`: 513→from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError 517→if isinstance(push_result, PushError): 518→ raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message) 523→- **Mid-logic errors**: Errors discovered during execution, not preconditions 524→- **Discriminated union errors**: When consuming `Result | Error` types from gateway layer 525→- **After operations fail**: When an operation returns an error variant 530→- `UserFacingCliError`: For errors during execution (e.g., git push fails) 531→- Both produce the same styled output (`Error: ` prefix in red) 533→- Ensure uses `UserFacingCliError` internally 538→- Mid-function operation errors → Raise `UserFacingCliError` 539→- Complex multi-step operations → Mix both (Ensure upfront, UserFacingCliError for failures) 575→For multi-step validations, use sequential Ensure calls with specific error messages: 578→# Multi-condition validation - each check has specific error 591→# Validation with early return - fail fast on first error 597→**Design Principle:** Prefer simple sequential checks over complex validation abstractions. Each check should have a specific, actionable error message. This aligns with the LBYL (Look Before You Leap) philosophy and makes code easier to understand and debug. 599→**Exit Codes:** All Ensure methods use exit code 1 for validation failures. This is consistent across all CLI commands. 607→1. **If error has a boolean condition** → Use `Ensure.invariant()` 612→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Something is wrong") 619→2. **If error returns a value when truthy** → Use `Ensure.truthy()` 625→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "No results found") 632→3. **If error checks for None specifically** → Use `Ensure.not_none()` 638→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Value is required") 645→4. **If error has no clear condition or needs custom flow** → Keep as direct pattern 649→**Pattern: Fallthrough/catch-all errors with no clear boolean condition** 651→Some errors occur as the "else" case after multiple checks have been exhausted. There's no meaningful boolean condition to express - the error state IS the remaining case. 664→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 670→**Why not migrate:** Using `Ensure.invariant(True, ...)` or wrapping with an artificial condition would be misleading. The error isn't about a condition being false - it's about reaching a catch-all state. 672→**Pattern: Errors with complex multi-line remediation messages** 674→When the error message spans multiple lines with detailed instructions, the `Ensure` API may not accommodate the formatting needs cleanly. 676→**Pattern: Errors that need conditional additional output before exit** 684→1. **Identify the error condition** - Is there a clear boolean/truthy/None check? 686→3. **Write the error message** - Follow the Error Message Guidelines (no "Error: " prefix) 687→4. **Test behavior is unchanged** - Error should trigger at the same conditions 697→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Already at the top...") 711→- The error message is a single line 755→| Action: Failed | `[red]Failed[/red]` | Execution failed | 850→This causes columns to appear misaligned whenever emoji are present. The misalignment is silent — code runs without errors but output looks wrong.

efc601fa2 Add plan: Plan: Add --json to erk pr list and erk pr view 547c3bf81 Push impl session 45f87f93-2301-49c7-ae93-33319d07eea8 for plan #9013 8a3cdbc87 Implement @json_output decorator and structured JSON output for one-shot command 498b828e3 Push impl session for plan #9013 619ac9669 Add plan for PR #9013 e38345930 Add plan: Plan: @json_output Decorator + Structured Errors + One-Shot JSON e386a44c6 Add plan: Learn: Fix changelog-commits JSON output size for agent consumption 298833493 Fix changelog-commits JSON output size for agent consumption (#9006) b07c8d3d3 Add plan: Learn: Add "unexpected end of JSON input" as transient error 69cad1081 Add "unexpected end of JSON input" as transient error (#8703) 6234cb13c Add plan: Add "unexpected end of JSON input" as transient error 2a00c9bea Delete .impl/issue.json legacy support and consolidate to plan-ref.json (#8388) 58cb68bc4 Update docs to reference .impl/plan-ref.json (with legacy .impl/issue.json fallback) (#8346) c02ca1001 Update docs to reference .impl/plan-ref.json (with legacy .impl/issue.json fallback) (#8346) 423313fff Update docs to reference .impl/plan-ref.json (with legacy .impl/issue.json fallback) (#8346) 3349b7589 Delete get_branch_issue() dead code and simplify to plan-ref.json (#8269) daf9c0975 Delete get_branch_issue() dead code and simplify to plan-ref.json (#8269) 97b902c37 Add plan: Documentation Plan: Delete get_branch_issue() dead code and simplify to plan-ref.json effa09215 Migrate impl test fixtures from issue.json to plan-ref.json format (#8122) 24a05826b Add plan: Documentation Plan: Remove plan ID encoding from branch names, migrate to plan-ref.json

session.tool_result:
commit 8a3cdbc874359413030ce75291f354a5b9d33676 Author: schrockn <schrockn@users.noreply.github.com> Date: Sun Mar 8 14:19:06 2026 +0000
Implement @json_output decorator and structured JSON output for one-shot command
This PR adds agent-friendly JSON output infrastructure to the CLI, enabling structured machine-readable results alongside human-readable output. The `@json_output` decorator provides uniform error serialization across commands, while `erk one-shot --json` emits detailed dispatch results or dry-run previews.
## Key Changes
- Add `@json_output` decorator (`src/erk/cli/json_output.py`) that appends `--json` flag to commands and catches `UserFacingCliError` exceptions, serializing them as JSON when the flag is active - Extend `UserFacingCliError` with optional `error_type` field (default: "cli_error") for classification of error conditions (auth_required, invalid_input, invalid_repo) - Replace dry-run `None` return with `OneShotDryRunResult` dataclass in `dispatch_one_shot_remote`, enabling JSON serialization of preview data - Wire `@json_output` onto `one_shot` command with JSON output branches for both dispatch success and dry-run scenarios, including PR/run URLs - Replace `Ensure.invariant` validation with `UserFacingCliError` raises to enable `error_type` classification
<details> <summary>Files Changed</summary>
### Added (3 files) - `src/erk/cli/json_output.py` - Decorator implementation and `emit_json()` helper for success results - `tests/unit/cli/test_json_output.py` - Unit tests for decorator flag handling, error serialization, and exception passthrough - `tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py` - Integration tests for one-shot JSON output (success, dry-run, error cases)
### Modified (6 files) - `src/erk/cli/ensure.py` - Add `error_type` kwarg to `UserFacingCliError.__init__` - `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py` - Add `@json_output`, JSON output branches, error type annotations - `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py` - Add `OneShotDryRunResult` dataclass, return from dry-run path - `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py` - Update dry-run checks to use `isinstance(result, OneShotDispatchResult)` - `src/erk/core/workflow_smoke_test.py` - Update dry-run validation to check result type
... [46 lines omitted] ...
{"success": false, "error_type": "invalid_input", "message": "Prompt must not be empty"} Agents can now parse structured JSON results while human users continue to see formatted console output. Error conditions include `error_type` for programmatic routing. - The `error_type` parameter is optional with sensible default ("cli_error") to avoid requiring updates to ~49 existing `UserFacingCliError` call sites

1→"""CLI error handling utilities with styled output. 2→ 3→This module provides the Ensure class for asserting invariants in CLI commands 4→with consistent, user-friendly error messages. All errors use red "Error:" prefix 5→for visual consistency. 6→ 7→Domain-Specific Methods: 8→- Git state validations (branch checks, worktree existence, clean state) 9→- Configuration validations (required fields, format checks) 10→- Argument validations (count, type, range) 11→- File/path validations (readable, writable, not hidden) 12→- String/collection validations (non-empty, non-null) 13→- External tool validations (gh CLI installed) 14→""" 15→ 16→from __future__ import annotations 17→ 18→import shutil 19→from pathlib import Path 20→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, TypeVar 21→ 22→import click 23→ 24→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.disabled import GraphiteDisabled, GraphiteDisabledError 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→ 27→if TYPE_CHECKING: 28→ from erk.core.context import ErkContext 29→ 30→T = TypeVar("T")
... [75 lines omitted] ...
33→class UserFacingCliError(click.ClickException): 34→ """Exception for user-facing CLI errors with styled output. 36→ Extends click.ClickException so Click catches it automatically at every 37→ level (groups, subgroups, commands) and converts it to a styled error 42→ raise UserFacingCliError("Not a GitHub repository") 43→ raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message) 51→ """Display styled error message to stderr.""" 52→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + self.format_message()) 56→ """Helper class for asserting invariants with consistent error handling.""" 59→ def invariant(condition: bool, error_message: str) -> None: 60→ """Ensure condition is true, otherwise output styled error and exit. 64→ error_message: Error message to display if condition is false. 65→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 68→ UserFacingCliError: If condition is false 71→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 74→ def truthy(value: T, error_message: str) -> T: 75→ """Ensure value is truthy, otherwise output styled error and exit. 79→ error_message: Error message to display if value is falsy. 80→ "Error: " prefix will be added automatically in red. 86→ UserFacingCliError: If value is falsy 89→ raise UserFacingCliError(error_message) 93→ def not_none(value: T | None, error_message: str) -> T: 94→ """Ensure value is not None, otherwise output styled error and exit.

1→"""Command to dispatch a task for fully autonomous remote execution. 2→ 3→No local planning — the remote Claude explores the codebase, plans the change, 4→implements it, and creates a PR. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk one-shot "fix the import in config.py" 8→ erk one-shot --file prompt.md 9→ erk one-shot "add type hints to utils.py" --model opus 10→ erk one-shot "fix the typo in README.md" --dry-run 11→ erk one-shot "rename issue_number to plan_number in impl_init.py" --plan-only 12→ erk one-shot "fix config bug" --repo owner/repo 13→""" 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk.cli.commands.implement_shared import normalize_model_name 20→from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_remote_dispatch import ( 21→ OneShotDispatchParams, 22→ dispatch_one_shot_remote, 23→) 24→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 25→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError 26→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 27→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 28→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.real import RealRemoteGitHub 29→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 30→
... [75 lines omitted] ...

1→# Plan: Add --json to erk pr list and erk pr view 2→ 3→**Part of Objective #9009, Node 2.1 (pr-read-json)** 4→**Depends on:** PR #9013 (nodes 1.1-1.3: `@json_output` decorator, structured errors, one-shot JSON) 5→ 6→## Context 7→ 8→Agents consuming `erk pr list` and `erk pr view` currently get human-readable Rich tables and styled text. The underlying data is already available as JSON via exec scripts (`dash-data` and `get-plan-info`), but the CLI commands don't expose it. This plan adds `--json` flags to both commands using the `@json_output` decorator from PR #9013, passing through the existing exec script JSON output. 9→ 10→## Step 1: Add `--json` to `erk pr list` 11→ 12→**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py` 13→ 14→Apply `@json_output` decorator to `pr_list` command. When `json_mode=True`, serialize the `PlanRowData` rows to JSON using the existing `_serialize_plan_row()` helper from `dash_data.py` and emit via `emit_json()`. 15→ 16→Changes: 17→- Import `json_output`, `emit_json` from `erk.cli.json_output` 18→- Import `_serialize_plan_row` from `erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.dash_data` (or extract to shared location) 19→- Add `@json_output` above `@click.command("list")` 20→- Add `json_mode: bool` parameter to `pr_list()` 21→- Pass `json_mode` into `_pr_list_impl()` (add parameter) 22→- In `_pr_list_impl()`, after sorting, if `json_mode`: call `emit_json({"plans": [_serialize_plan_row(r) for r in rows], "count": len(rows)})` and return 23→ 24→**Decision: Reuse `_serialize_plan_row`** — Extract it from `dash_data.py` to a shared module (`src/erk/tui/data/serialization.py`) so both `dash_data.py` and `list_cmd.py` import from the same place. This avoids duplication and keeps the output format identical. 25→ 26→### Output shape (matches `dash-data` exec script): 27→```json 28→{"success": true, "plans": [{...PlanRowData fields...}], "count": 5} 29→``` 30→
... [90 lines omitted] ...

"""JSON output decorator for CLI commands.
Adds a --json flag to Click commands and handles error serialization. Commands handle success JSON inline via emit_json(); the decorator only handles the error path uniformly by catching UserFacingCliError. """
import json from typing import Any
import click
from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError
def json_output(cmd: click.Command) -> click.Command: """Add --json flag and JSON error handling to a Click command.
Must be applied ABOVE @click.command (i.e., listed above it in the decorator stack). This is because decorators are applied bottom-to-top, so @json_output runs AFTER @click.command creates the Command object.
When --json is passed: - UserFacingCliError is caught and serialized as JSON to stdout - SystemExit and other exceptions pass through unchanged
When --json is not passed: - Delegates to the original callback unchanged
Usage:
... [56 lines omitted] ...
# Wrap the callback to intercept errors in JSON mode except UserFacingCliError as exc: error_data = { "error_type": exc.error_type, click.echo(json.dumps(error_data))

150→ table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold") 151→ 152→ # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns() 153→ table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6) 154→ table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8) 155→ table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4) 156→ table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7) 157→ table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5) 158→ table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3) 159→ table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42) 160→ table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10) 161→ table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3) 162→ table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9) 163→ if show_pr_column: 164→ table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10) 165→ table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8) 166→ table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5) 167→ 168→ for row in rows: 169→ values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column) 170→ table.add_row(*values) 171→ 172→ return table 173→ 174→ 175→def _row_to_static_values( 176→ row: PlanRowData, 177→ *, 178→ show_pr_column: bool, 179→) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
... [225 lines omitted] ...
